---
title: "Quiz"
format: html

previous:
  href: "User-Citing and Attribution.qmd"
  text: "Citing and Attribution"

next:
  href: "../OERs for creators/Creator-learning outcomes.qmd"
  text: "OERs for Creators"
---

<form id="quiz">

<!-- Q1 -->

<p><strong>1) When using OERs in a UK university, which of the following is typically required to meet attribution standards?</strong><br> <label><input type="radio" name="q1" value="A" data-correct="true"> A) Title, creator, source, and licence</label><br> <label><input type="radio" name="q1" value="B"> B) The title of the work</label><br> <label><input type="radio" name="q1" value="C"> C) Only the creator's name and date</label><br> <label><input type="radio" name="q1" value="D"> D) Only the licence type</label></p>

<!-- Q2 -->

<p><strong>2) A non-profit organisation wants to reuse a work licensed under CC BY-NC. Which of the following uses would comply with the licence?</strong><br> <label><input type="radio" name="q2" value="A"> A) Using the work as part of a fundraising activity</label><br> <label><input type="radio" name="q2" value="B" data-correct="true"> B) Using the work in free internal training</label><br> <label><input type="radio" name="q2" value="C"> C) Using the work in fee-based external training</label><br> <label><input type="radio" name="q2" value="D"> D) All of the above</label></p>

<!-- Q3 -->

<p><strong>3) Are OERs more trustworthy than commercial resources?</strong><br> <label><input type="radio" name="q3" value="A"> A) No, they are generally of a lower quality</label><br> <label><input type="radio" name="q3" value="B" data-correct="true"> B) No, you should apply critical tests of quality, accuracy, and completeness to all resources</label><br> <label><input type="radio" name="q3" value="C"> C) Yes, they are generally created by more trustworthy people</label><br> <label><input type="radio" name="q3" value="D"> D) Yes, they're public and so they are continuously updated</label></p>

<!-- Hyperlinked controls -->

<p><a href="#" id="submit-link">✔️ Submit</a>  </p>

</form>

<!-- Result area -->

::: {#result style="font-weight: bold; margin-top: 1em;"}
:::

```{=html}
<script>
document.getElementById("submit-link").addEventListener("click", function (e) {
  e.preventDefault();

  const form = document.getElementById("quiz");
  const correct = form.querySelectorAll('input[data-correct="true"]:checked').length;
  const total = 3;

  const result = document.getElementById("result");
  const indexLink = document.getElementById("index-link");
  const backLink = document.getElementById("back-link");

  let message = `You scored ${correct}/${total}. `;

  if (correct >= 2) {
    message += "Well done!";
    indexLink.style.display = "block";
    backLink.style.display = "none";
  } else {
    message += "You might want to go back and look at sections of the Introduction to OERs unit.";
    indexLink.style.display = "none";
    backLink.style.display = "block";
  }

  result.textContent = message;
});
</script>
```

::: {#index-link style="display: none; margin-top: 1.5em; text-align: center;"}
<a href="/index.html"> <img src="images/clipboard-3722003544.png" alt="Return to programme home page" width="50"/> </a>
:::

::: {#back-link style="display: none; margin-top: 1.5em; text-align: center;"}
<a href="/OERs for users/User-learning outcomes.html"> <img src="images/clipboard-372805318.png" alt="Return to the beginning of the unit" width="50"/> </a>
:::

------------------------------------------------------------------------

::: nav-footer
```{=html}
<a href="{{< meta previous.href >}}" aria-label="Go to previous page: {{< meta previous.text >}}">
  <span aria-hidden="true">«</span> {{< meta previous.text >}}
</a>

<a href="{{< meta next.href >}}" aria-label="Go to next page: {{< meta next.text >}}">
  {{< meta next.text >}} <span aria-hidden="true">»</span>
</a>
```
:::
